mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals improving labor market conditions, which raises expectations of future Fed tightening and higher term premiums, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 20-day yield change is non-positive, or a long-short portfolio based on PAYEMS momentum fails to produce positive Sharpe ratio over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales (RSAFS) grow faster than 0.5% month-over-month, it signals strong consumer demand that boosts economic growth expectations and inflation fears, leading to higher 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Median forward yield change following trigger events is not significantly above zero at the 5% level over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signals that the Fed may need to tighten policy, compressing bond prices and raising yields; the effect is amplified when both series increase over a rolling 3-month window.
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient in a linear regression of yield changes on CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding supply capacity and economic vitality, which lifts long-term growth expectations and the neutral real rate, causing the 10-year yield to increase; this effect is most pronounced during economic expansion regimes.
failure_condition: Average forward yield change in expansion periods identified by NBER dates is not positively correlated with INDPRO growth at the 0.05 significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that pressures the Fed to raise rates sooner, increasing term premiums and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward yield changes after triggering below 6.0% are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) in a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) reflect robust residential investment and consumer confidence, boosting GDP growth expectations and real yields over a short horizon; this effect is immediate and fades quickly, best captured over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-business-day correlation between HOUST surprises and subsequent yield changes is not reliably positive across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), further accommodation is limited, so inflation surprises (CPIAUCSL) become the primary driver of yield movements; higher inflation erodes real returns and pushes nominal yields up over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During ZLB periods, the correlation between CPIAUCSL surprises and subsequent yield changes is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in both real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirms broad-based demand expansion, reinforcing expectations of sustained growth and higher inflation, which pushes the 10-year yield up over a longer horizon (60 trading days) as the economy absorbs slack.
failure_condition: The difference in forward yield changes between periods when both series are above trend versus below trend is not statistically significant (two-sample t-test p > 0.05).
